introSection: Figure Eight was a wooden roller coaster located at Spanish City Amusement Park in Whitley Bay, Tyne & Wear, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The Figure Eight was supplied by T. M. Harton Co. of Pittsburg, USA and was opened in 1909.

The coaster was demolished in early 1974.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On 21 August 1909, a man was thrown from one of the cars as it turned a corner and fell on to the ride structure below. He suffered a concussion, fractured ribs, a bruised hip, and an injured ear.

fullText:
Figure Eight was a wooden roller coaster located at Spanish City Amusement Park in Whitley Bay, Tyne & Wear, England, UK.

The Figure Eight was supplied by T. M. Harton Co. of Pittsburg, USA and was opened in 1909.

The coaster was demolished in early 1974.

On 21 August 1909, a man was thrown from one of the cars as it turned a corner and fell on to the ride structure below. He suffered a concussion, fractured ribs, a bruised hip, and an injured ear.
